events.title: Sodium aluminate spill into the Cumberland River near ALCOA facility in Nashville
events.description: Reports state roughly 130,000 pounds of sodium aluminate were spilled at/near an ALCOA Specialty Chemicals facility on the Cumberland River after a loading-area manifold/pipe was left open following a transfer. The material reportedly entered the river, prompting monitoring and cleanup and a temporary river traffic closure; cleanup was reported complete by October 2.

key_quotes.quote: approximately 130,000 pounds of sodium aluminate ... entered the Cumberland River

dedupe.cluster_hint: NOAA IncidentNews sodium aluminate Cumberland River Nashville Oct 1992

events.title: Greenhill Petroleum well blowout and oil spill affecting waters near East Timbalier Island, Louisiana
events.description: A Greenhill Petroleum development well blowout that began in late September continued into early October, with oil slicking and reports of shoreline oiling toward East Timbalier Island. The well was reported brought under control/killed by October 9, with burning used to remove some oil and response/cleanup continuing afterward.

sources.headline: Blowout brought under control in Gulf of Mexico

dedupe.cluster_hint: Greenhill Petroleum blowout East Timbalier Island Oct 1992

events.title: Reported platform collision and minor oil slick in the Mississippi River Delta main channel
events.description: A report states a tug/barge struck an offshore well platform in the Mississippi River Delta’s main channel and an MMS aircraft observed an oil slick of about three miles. Initial reports listed unknown source and quantity, with later notes indicating the oil likely came from a leaking valve and impacts were considered minor with limited shoreline sheen reported.

dedupe.cluster_hint: Mississippi River Delta platform collision slick Oct 1992 NOAA 6907

events.title: Hurricane Virgil brings heavy rain and flooding risk to southwestern Mexico
events.description: Contemporary reporting said Tropical Storm Virgil was downgraded and continued to bring heavy rain to Mexicos Pacific coast, raising concerns about flooding and mudslides in affected areas.

key_quotes.quote: Virgil was downgraded to a tropical storm... heavy rains... could cause flooding and mudslides.

events.title: Hurricane Winifred produces heavy rainfall in western Mexico
events.description: NOAA/WPC documentation for Hurricane Winifred (1992) provides rainfall information associated with the storm affecting western Mexico during October 1992.

key_quotes.quote: winifred1992.html (WPC tropical rainfall page) documents rainfall associated with Hurricane Winifred (1992).

events.title: Typhoon threatens southern Thailand after crossing Vietnam, prompting flood and wave warnings
events.description: Contemporary reporting said a typhoon moved across Vietnam and then threatened southern Thailand, prompting evacuations and warnings for flooding and large waves due to heavy rain and winds.

key_quotes.quote: Typhoon threatens southern Thailand... authorities warned of floods and large waves... evacuations were ordered.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1992 Typhoon (Angela/Osang) Vietnam to Thailand flooding/wave warnings

events.title: Dahshur (near Cairo), Egypt earthquake (Oct 12, 1992)
events.description: A damaging earthquake struck near Dahshur, south of Cairo, and caused extensive building damage and casualties across Cairo and northeastern Egypt. The event is documented by USGS publications for the October 12, 1992 Dahshur earthquake.

location.name: Near Dahshur village, south of Cairo, Egypt

sources.headline: USGS publication record for the October 12, 1992 Dahshur, Egypt earthquake

events.title: Northwestern Colombia (Murindó area) earthquake (Oct 18, 1992)
events.description: A major earthquake struck near Murindó in northwestern Colombia, damaging buildings in the region and causing injuries and fatalities. Contemporary reporting described impacts in villages near the epicentral area.

sources.headline: Northwestern Colombia was shaken by an earthquake for the ...

key_quotes.quote: Northwestern Colombia was shaken by an earthquake

events.title: Northwestern Colombia (Murindó area) strong earthquake (Oct 17, 1992)
events.description: A strong earthquake struck the Murindó area in northwestern Colombia, collapsing homes and damaging buildings and leaving many people without shelter. Reporting described it as a significant quake preceding the larger October 18 event sequence.

location.name: Murindó area, Antioquia region, northwestern Colombia

events.title: Air tanker crash ignites secondary fire during Cleveland Fire (Union Valley Reservoir area, California)
events.description: During suppression of the Cleveland Fire in the El Dorado National Forest area, a DC-7 air tanker crashed on Oct. 1, 1992. The crash reportedly ignited a second fire that spread to about 500 acres and temporarily halted air operations.

location.name: Near Union Valley Reservoir / El Dorado National Forest area, El Dorado County, California, USA

sources.headline: Air tanker crashes in California forest fire

key_quotes.quote: ignited a second blaze that spread to about 500 acres

events.title: Cleveland Fire reaches full containment (El Dorado National Forest / Highway 50 corridor, California)
events.description: The Cleveland Fire burning in the El Dorado National Forest along the U.S. Route 50 corridor was reported as fully contained on Oct. 4, 1992. The fire had led to evacuations and highway closures during its run.

location.name: El Dorado County (U.S. Route 50 corridor west of South Lake Tahoe), California, USA

events.title: Arson-suspected brush fire threatens Ventura oil field and psychiatric hospital (Ventura, California)
events.description: A brush fire reported Oct. 1, 1992 burned more than 450 acres near Ventura, California. It reportedly threatened nearby residences, the Vista del Mar Psychiatric Hospital, and infrastructure in a Texaco oil field; officials treated the blaze as arson-related and sought a person for questioning.

events.title: Greenhill Petroleum well blowout oils shoreline at East Timbalier Island (Louisiana)
events.description: During the Greenhill Petroleum well blowout incident in Timbalier Bay, reports documented oil slicking toward East Timbalier Island and shoreline oiling along parts of the island in early October 1992. The incident implied direct impacts to coastal habitat and wildlife resources in the area.

location.name: East Timbalier Island / Timbalier Bay, Louisiana, USA

sources.headline: Eight workers injured fighting Gulf oil spill

key_quotes.quote: Eight workers injured fighting Gulf oil spill

dedupe.cluster_hint: Greenhill Petroleum well blowout Timbalier Bay Oct 1992

events.title: Wildlife rehabilitation response reported for Timbalier Bay oil spill (Louisiana)
events.description: A wildlife response and rehabilitation effort for an oil spill was documented as part of organizational response history, indicating treatment/handling of oiled birds associated with the Timbalier Bay incident during October 1992. This reflects biodiversity impacts requiring intervention during the spill period.

events.title: U.S. law establishes Rocky Mountain Arsenal National Wildlife Refuge (Colorado)
events.description: A U.S. law signed on October 9, 1992 established the Rocky Mountain Arsenal National Wildlife Refuge near Denver, creating a conservation unit intended to protect and manage habitat for wildlife on former arsenal lands alongside ongoing remediation considerations. The action formalized long-term habitat protection objectives for the site.

events.title: Wild Bird Conservation Act signed, restricting U.S. imports of wild-caught birds
events.description: A U.S. law signed on October 27, 1992 restricted imports of certain wild-caught birds, aiming to reduce trade pressures on wild populations and support conservation outcomes. The policy has biodiversity implications beyond the U.S. through impacts on international wildlife trade.

events.title: Rocky Mountain Arsenal National Wildlife Refuge Act signed (Public Law 102-402)
events.description: President George H. W. Bush signed legislation establishing the Rocky Mountain Arsenal National Wildlife Refuge. The law set a framework for the site’s transition into the National Wildlife Refuge System following cleanup and remediation.

location.name: Rocky Mountain Arsenal near Denver, Colorado, United States

sources.source_name: George H.W. Bush Presidential Library and Museum
sources.headline: Public Papers: 4942
H.R.1435 - Rocky Mountain Arsenal National Wildlife Refuge Act of 1992

key_quotes.quote: signed legislation creating the Rocky Mountain Arsenal National Wildlife Refuge

events.title: EPA final rule listing chlorinated toluenes production wastes under RCRA and assigning CERCLA reportable quantities
events.description: EPA issued a final action amending RCRA regulations to add chlorinated-toluene production wastes to the hazardous waste list and to include associated hazardous constituents. The action also designated the listed wastes as CERCLA hazardous substances with reportable quantities.

location.name: United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), United States

sources.headline: Hazardous Waste Management System; Identification and Listing of Hazardous Waste and CERCLA Hazardous Substance Designations and RQs (chlorinated toluenes production wastes)

key_quotes.quote: adding three chlorinated-toluene production wastes to the hazardous waste list

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA RCRA/CERCLA chlorinated toluenes wastes final action Oct 1992

events.title: Energy Policy Act of 1992 signed into law
events.description: President George H. W. Bush signed the Energy Policy Act of 1992, a major U.S. energy statute. The act included provisions aimed at improving energy efficiency and guiding national energy policy implementation.

events.title: EPA removes expiration date from reinstated RCRA mixture and derived-from rules
events.description: EPA removed the April 28, 1993 expiration date from its reinstatement of the RCRA hazardous waste mixture and derived-from rules. The action aimed to preserve continuity in the national hazardous waste program while EPA evaluated potential modifications.

location.name: United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), United States

sources.headline: Hazardous Waste Management System; Definition of Hazardous Waste; Mixture and Derived-From Rules (Federal Register)

key_quotes.quote: removing the expiration date from the reinstated RCRA “mixture” and “derived-from” rules

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA RCRA mixture/derived-from expiration date removed Oct 1992

events.title: Water Resources Development Act of 1992 enacted (Public Law 102-580)
events.description: The Water Resources Development Act of 1992 became law, authorizing and guiding U.S. Army Corps of Engineers civil works activities. The act also included a range of related U.S. water-resources policy provisions.

sources.headline: H.R.6167 - Water Resources Development Act of 1992
